name: United States (presidential standard)
description: A dark blue field bearing in the centre the Seal of the President of the United States: a bald eagle with outstretched wings clasping an olive branch in its dexter talon and a bundle of thirteen arrows in its sinister, with a striped (red and white) shield on its breast surmounted by a blue chief, holding in its beak a scroll inscribed E PLURIBUS UNUM, surrounded by a ring of fifty white five-pointed stars.
history: The modern presidential flag was adopted by President Truman on 25 October 1945, replacing the 1916 design. A fiftieth star was added in 1959 with the admission of Hawaii.

features.role: Seal of the President of the United States
features.symbolism: an eagle clutching olive branch and arrows, with the shield of the United States on its breast

features.role: the fifty states surrounding the seal
